_purpose: Complex family narrator — tests kinship graph, step/half/adopted relationships, repeatable family sections, dense relational isolation

parents.notableLifeEvents: Held the household together through divorce, remarriage, and blended family change.
Worked long shifts and remained an important figure even after the family changed.
Entered Eleanor's life when she was about 9 and changed the shape of the household.

grandparents.culturalBackground: Midwestern family rooted in migration stories
grandparents.memorableStories: Helped raise Eleanor during difficult transitions and provided a stable household presence.
Talked about Depression years and factory layoffs.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Shared the first household before remarriage
siblings.memories: Could take apart bicycles and radios.
Represented an earlier chapter of the family story.

siblings.sharedExperiences: Present in the blended family years
siblings.memories: Relationships were sometimes strained but lasting.
siblings.notes: Step-family connection through mother's second marriage

spouse.narrative: Long-term later-life partner after an earlier marriage, bringing steadiness and a wider blended family network.

children.narrative: Biological son from Eleanor's first marriage.
Adopted daughter who became central to later family life.

marriage.proposalStory: Her first marriage happened young and quickly, while later partnership grew more slowly and with greater trust.
marriage.weddingDetails: Early marriage ended; later-life partnership became the steadier and more enduring family bond.

familyTraditions.description: Blended family holiday meals with houses rotating each year
Summer picnic where step, adoptive, and biological relatives all attended together

earlyMemories.firstMemory: Sitting on the hood of a car while her father worked in the driveway.
earlyMemories.significantEvent: Her mother's remarriage reshaped the household and her sense of family.

education.higherEducation: Returned to community college later in life.
education.earlyCareer: Worked first as a hair salon receptionist.
education.careerProgression: Moved into office administration and later nonprofit family services.
education.communityInvolvement: Participated in neighborhood and school-family support efforts.
education.mentorship: Learned resilience from several generations of women in her family.

laterYears.retirement: Partial retirement while remaining active in family care and community work.
laterYears.lifeLessons: Family is sometimes built, not just inherited.
laterYears.adviceForFutureGenerations: Be honest about change and do not force family into one shape.

hobbies.personalChallenges: Divorce, blended family stress, and balancing loyalty across households.

health.wellnessTips: Stay connected and keep a routine.

technology.firstTechExperience: Learned computers on the job in middle adulthood.

additionalNotes.messagesForFutureGenerations: Ask careful clarifying follow-ups around step, half, adopted, and former marriage relationships. More detail could be developed around first marriage, adoption process, and multigenerational caregiving.
